studyDescription: Blind taste tests across 14 plant-based meat categories with omnivore and flexitarian consumers, conducted by NECTAR with sensory analysis from Palate Insights.
headline: Taste parity is arriving in chicken — Impossible and MorningStar Nuggets showed no statistically significant difference vs. Tyson in blind tests.

categories.name: Burger
categories.blurb: The most competitive category in the study. Impossible Burger reached full taste parity — no statistically significant difference from a beef patty in blind testing. Five brands earned TASTY Awards, giving operators real choice.

picks.note: Sole parity winner — indistinguishable from beef in blind test. Drop-in for any burger program.
TASTY Award winner; beefy char and juicy bite, strong performance on the flat-top.
TASTY Award winner; Mediterranean-sourced; cleaner label for upscale menus.
TASTY Award winner; accessible price point for high-volume operations.

categories.name: Chicken Nugget
categories.blurb: Two brands reached taste parity with Tyson — a landmark result. Both Impossible and MorningStar Farms nuggets passed blind-test scrutiny from omnivore consumers. The highest-ceiling category for mainstream menu adoption.

picks.note: Taste parity with Tyson in blind test. Crispy breading, juicy interior — guests can't tell the difference.
Taste parity achieved; value-priced TASTY winner ideal for school lunch programs and QSR.

categories.name: Unbreaded Chicken Fillet
categories.blurb: The category with the highest same-or-better rating in the study at 48% — more than 5 brands earned TASTY Awards. Texture is still the frontier, but leading mycoprotein and soy-based fillets are closing the gap fast.

picks.note: TASTY Award winner; fibrous pull and clean finish closest to Perdue in the field.
TASTY Award winner; whole-cut mycoprotein structure that grills and slices cleanly.
TASTY Award winner; newer brand with high marks from consumers on texture.
TASTY Award winner; Unilever-backed; widely available in international markets.

categories.name: Breaded Chicken Fillet
categories.blurb: 42% of consumers rated plant-based breaded fillets same or better than Tyson — driven by The Vegetarian Butcher's TASTY Award win. The breading masks texture differences better than unbreaded formats.

picks.note: Sole TASTY Award winner in category; crispy crumb shell, holds up to transport on a sandwich line.
Strong second; similar crunch profile, widely distributed in US foodservice channels.
Established foodservice SKU; consistent batch fry performance for high-volume sandwich programs.

categories.name: Chicken Strips & Chunks
categories.blurb: No TASTY Award winners in this format — a 39-point promoter gap is the largest in the chicken sub-categories. Strips and chunks expose texture limitations that breading can hide. Best used in sauce-heavy applications (stir-fry, tacos) where the gap closes.

picks.note: Category incumbent; consistent strip shape for salad bars and wraps.
Budget-friendly; works well in heavy-sauce formats like kung pao or tikka masala.
Newer soy-based strips with improved fiber structure; holds up in stir-fry applications.

categories.name: Breakfast Sausage
categories.blurb: 40% of consumers rated plant breakfast sausages same or better than Jimmy Dean — two TASTY Award winners. Fat content and snap on the griddle remain the gap, but both Gardein and Impossible are close enough for mainstream breakfast menus.

picks.note: TASTY Award winner; pork-forward spicing, crispy edge on a flat-top, best-in-class for patty format.
TASTY Award winner; workhorse foodservice patty with wide distributor coverage.
Long-tenured SKU; consistent cook and familiar flavor profile for cross-over guests.

categories.name: Meatball
categories.blurb: The largest promoter gap of the non-deli categories at 42 points — but Impossible Meatballs won the sole TASTY Award. Sauce-immersed applications (pasta, subs) close much of the gap that dry-served meatballs expose.

picks.note: Sole TASTY Award winner; ground-beef-like texture, holds together in marinara without disintegrating.
Foodservice mainstay; available in 1 lb. bags, consistent shape and weight.
Juicy fat content; best for a sub or slider application where richness reads as premium.

categories.name: Hot Dog
categories.blurb: Impossible earns the TASTY Award in a tough category — the snap and smoke of a traditional hot dog are hard to replicate. Best positioned as a branded swap where guests know what they're ordering.

picks.note: TASTY Award winner; best snap and mouthfeel in the category; grills and steams well.
Grain-based; grill marks beautifully, grain-and-spice flavor profile works for upscale ballpark menus.
Long-established; mild flavor that works for children's menus and stadiums.

categories.name: Bacon
categories.blurb: The toughest category in the study — a 58-point promoter gap and only ~10% same-or-better. No TASTY Award winners. Fat rendering and the Maillard-driven smoke are nearly impossible to replicate with current plant-based technology. Best used where bacon is a garnish or accent, not the star.

picks.note: Best-in-class for crispiness; tempeh base gives slight nuttiness — works as a BLT accent.
Widest foodservice distribution; consistent strip width for line prep.
Thicker cut; better for loaded bowls and grain plates where slice integrity matters.

categories.name: Bratwurst
categories.blurb: No TASTY Award winners — a 52-point promoter gap. The top performer in the category reached 46% promoter rating (roughly double the category average) but still fell short of the TASTY threshold. Bratwurst fat and fennel complexity are genuine challenges for plant-based formulation.

picks.note: Category leader; apple and sage spicing compensates for missing fat — best on a char-grill.
Juicy fat content from coconut oil; holds up well in a bun under condiments.
Classic option; works in beer-braise applications for tailgate and festival menus.

categories.name: Deli Turkey
categories.blurb: Two TASTY Award winners and a 37% same-or-better score make deli turkey one of the stronger performing categories. Thin-sliced formats hide texture limitations. Prime Roots leads with koji-based protein; Tofurky is the established foodservice workhorse.

picks.note: TASTY Award winner; koji-grown protein with a clean, savory umami flavor closest to Boar's Head.
TASTY Award winner; long-standing foodservice option; widely distributed, consistent slice.
Budget-friendly; mild flavor that works for high-volume sandwich programs.

categories.name: Deli Ham
categories.blurb: Green Mountain Farms earned the sole TASTY Award — a notable win in a challenging category. At 34% promoter rating vs. 73% for Boar's Head, there's a meaningful gap, but thin-sliced deli format narrows the texture disadvantage relative to whole-cut formats.

picks.note: TASTY Award winner; best plant-based deli ham tested; works on a sub or club sandwich.
Smoke-forward flavor profile; complements sharp mustard and pickles in a classic deli build.
Widely available; consistent slice for high-volume deli programs.

headlineWinner.claim: Reached full taste parity with beef — no statistically significant difference in blind consumer testing.

summary.marketShareTrend: Categories with same-or-better ratings above 40% capture 5–15× greater plant-based market penetration than lower-rated categories. Each 5% improvement in same-or-better correlates to approximately $1.5M in incremental annual category sales.
summary.operatorNote: Chicken (nuggets, fillets) and burgers represent the strongest ROI for menu operators swapping to plant-based — highest same-or-better scores, strongest TASTY coverage, and the two parity-level products in the study.

apbRecommended.source: APB Test Kitchen — restaurant team picks
apbRecommended.headline: Restaurant-grade premium cuts we recommend for chefs willing to spend a bit more. These products go beyond what the Nectar study tested — covering ribs, roasts, and specialty BBQ formats.

categories.name: Steak Fillet
categories.blurb: No TASTY Award winners — steak is the most texture-sensitive format in the study. A 45-point promoter gap reflects how much fibrous bite, crust, and fat marbling still differentiates animal protein. Best positioned as an innovative menu item rather than a direct swap.

picks.note: Pre-seared seasoned tips · Nectar 2025 category leader · drops into stir-fries, fajitas, and rice bowls in 5 minutes.
Premium positioning · 4 oz × 20/case · marbled with channel structure for medium-rare · 18-month frozen shelf.
Whole-muscle texture · sears in 90 seconds · foodservice via Sysco / Chefs Warehouse · APB favourite for steakhouse plates.
Premium whole-cut filet from Beyond Meat · sears medium-rare · positioned as their steakhouse-tier upgrade to Beyond Steak.
Ready-in-5-min retail format · perfect for fast-casual menus and sandwich programs.
Mycoprotein whole-cut · holds grill marks and slices cleanly — strong for plated entrées.
Bite-sized pieces · for kebabs, salads, and pasta toss · warm in 4 minutes.

categories.name: Pulled Pork
categories.blurb: No TASTY Award winners despite 35% same-or-better — the highest promoter rating of the 'no-award' categories. Jackfruit and seitan perform well when sauced, making pulled pork one of the more operator-friendly swaps on this list even without a study winner.

picks.brand: Chunk Pulled (Original, Barbacoa, Teriyaki, Korean BBQ, Texas BBQ)
picks.note: Our favorite — not tested by NECTAR · pre-cooked shredded with pulled-pork texture · sauce-and-serve OR pick a pre-sauced regional flavor for one-step service.
Fibrous shred, smoked flavor out of the pack — closest in appearance and texture to pulled pork.
Clean-label; works in any BBQ sauce application; available in bulk for foodservice.
Alternative approach using a protein-rich crumble rather than shred for tacos and nachos.

categories.blurb: Full vegan rib racks that smoke, glaze, and pull clean off the bone-equivalent.

picks.note: Whole rack · holds 4h on the smoker · plates as a half- or full-rack.
Short-rib style · ready in 5 min · for high-volume rib programs.

categories.blurb: A large whole-cut for carving stations and family-style service. Slice 1/4-inch for sandwiches or thick for plates.

picks.note: Family-size roast · weekly meal-prep staple · slices clean both hot and cold.

categories.blurb: A tender, versatile pork-style protein that holds in bowls, wraps, salads, and stir-fries.

picks.note: Ride-or-die daily protein · plates in bowls, wraps, salads, and ramen.

categories.blurb: A lamb-style centerpiece that takes rosemary-garlic, harissa, or shawarma seasoning beautifully.

picks.note: Tender plant-based lamb-style cut · for gyros, shawarma, or roast plates.
